subject: Gaming
title: Street Fighter II Creates a Genre
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Street Fighter II Creates a Genre.

hook.text: Street Fighter II created a new genre overnight. By 1993, it had earned $1.5 billion in quarters.

buildup.text: Capcom's game let two players fight head-to-head with unique characters and special moves.

discovery.text: Players discovered hidden combo systems by accident. Capcom noticed and made combos a core feature.

twist.text: Arcades became boxing rings where reputations were won or lost in seconds. Competition drove obsession.

climax.text: Street Fighter II spawned an entire genre and the template fighting games still follow today.

punchline.text: Accidental combos became intentional. A genre born from a glitch.

quiz.question: How were combos discovered in Street Fighter II?
options: Players found them by accident and Capcom made them official
Capcom designed them from the start
A strategy guide writer invented them
